metrics.name: Raw data line count
metrics.definition: Universal-newline line count of the source CSV minus the header row.
metrics.rationale: Anchor for every downstream completeness ratio. Without an authoritative raw count, no ingestion KPI is defensible.

metrics.name: Records ingested after P1 repair
metrics.definition: Rows in cleaned_p1_final.tsv after structural repair + value normalisation.
metrics.rationale: Records the surveillance system actually sees. Direct analogue to the JPM-case completeness gap.

metrics.name: Parse rate vs raw lines
metrics.definition: M1.2 / M1.1 * 100. The headline ingestion KPI.
metrics.rationale: If this is below 95% the vendor will silently drop records. The remaining 3.30% on this dataset are continuation lines absorbed into multi-line cells (NOT lost).

metrics.name: Quarantine bucket size after P1
metrics.definition: Rows that the P1 pipeline could not repair and chose to quarantine for manual review.
metrics.rationale: Quarantined records are deferred surveillance gaps — every quarantined row is a row no rule will fire on.

metrics.name: Bare-CR lines (silent-drop risk)
metrics.definition: Lines terminated by a bare \r rather than \r\n. Strict-LF readers (some legacy Java/C parsers, Unix wc) silently miss these.
metrics.target: 0 in the FUTURE (producer should emit CRLF only)
metrics.rationale: On THIS file the P1 pipeline normalises bare-CR -> CRLF, so no records are lost downstream. But the producer should fix the line-ending mix at source so every consumer sees the same record count.

metrics.name: CRLF-terminated lines
metrics.definition: Lines terminated by \r\n (standard Windows + RFC-4180 expectation).
metrics.rationale: Reported alongside M1.5 so a reader can reconcile any line-counting tool against the file.

metrics.name: ExchangeId populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in ExchangeId / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for venue-level surveillance + best-execution analysis.

metrics.name: MessageType populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in MessageType / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Cannot route a row to a surveillance rule without knowing what kind of event it is.

metrics.name: TransactionTime populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in TransactionTime / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for every time-windowed rule (cancel ratios, wash-trade timing, lifecycle ordering).

metrics.name: MessageDate populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in MessageDate / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Partition key for trading-day surveillance scope.

metrics.name: MessageId populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in MessageId / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Primary key. Nulls cannot be deduplicated, reconciled, or referenced by Cancel/Fill events.

metrics.name: LinkMessageId populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in LinkMessageId / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Optional — null is valid for parent New Orders. Only flagged when populated AND unresolvable (see M3.1).

metrics.name: ParentOrderId populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in ParentOrderId / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Optional — null is valid for parent New Orders. Only flagged when populated AND unresolvable (see M3.2).

metrics.name: Instrument populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in Instrument / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for every per-instrument rule (price outlier, position aggregation).

metrics.name: ISIN populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in ISIN / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for cross-venue / cross-system instrument joins.

metrics.name: BuyOrSell populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in BuyOrSell / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for wash-trade detection, position direction, side-imbalance rules.

metrics.name: Price populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in Price / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for price-outlier detection + best-execution. Some Cancel events may legitimately omit price; investigate the breakdown.

metrics.name: TotalVolume populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in TotalVolume / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for over-fill detection + position size + block-trade flagging.

metrics.name: Account populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in Account / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for account-level wash-trade and position-limit rules.

metrics.name: CounterPartyFirm populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in CounterPartyFirm / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for inter-firm market-abuse detection + counterparty risk.

metrics.name: Trader populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in Trader / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Reg-best-execution + MAR market-abuse attribution. Currently 64.87% of FILLS lack a Trader (see M6).

metrics.name: TransactionSource populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in TransactionSource / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for source attribution if the surveillance system aggregates multiple feeds.

metrics.name: Currency populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in Currency / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Required for FX normalisation if multi-currency.

metrics.name: Flags populated rate
metrics.definition: 100 - (nulls in Flags / total rows * 100)
metrics.rationale: Optional — present only on exception messages. Null is the expected state on the majority of rows.

metrics.name: Orphaned LinkMessageId rate
metrics.definition: P2 finding A1 / count(LinkMessageId is non-null) * 100. References to MessageIds that don't exist in the file.
metrics.rationale: Every orphaned reference is a child event the surveillance system cannot tie to its order. Lifecycle reconstruction breaks for those rows.

metrics.name: Orphaned ParentOrderId rate
metrics.definition: P2 finding A2 / count(ParentOrderId is non-null) * 100. References to MessageIds that don't exist in the file.
metrics.rationale: Breaks the textbook 'aggregate Fill <= parent volume' check (see M4.2). Producer must guarantee parent records are emitted before children.

metrics.name: Orphan-by-omission rate (children with NO upstream ref)
metrics.definition: P2 finding A3 / count(child events) * 100. Cancel/Replace/Fill/Reject rows where BOTH LinkMessageId AND ParentOrderId are null.
metrics.rationale: Hardest class of completeness gap — these rows cannot be tied to ANY order. A regulator audit will fail on these.

metrics.name: Schema misuse rate (New Order with parent)
metrics.definition: P2 finding A4 / count(New Order rows) * 100. New Orders carrying LinkMessageId or ParentOrderId.
metrics.rationale: Either MessageType is mislabelled or the parent column is mis-populated. Either way the surveillance system cannot trust the type label on these rows.

metrics.name: Time-travel rate (child before parent)
metrics.definition: P2 finding B1 / count(children with resolvable parent) * 100. Child event timestamp < parent timestamp.
metrics.rationale: Physically impossible. Indicates either timestamp corruption (clock skew at the producer) or misassigned parent reference.

metrics.name: Over-fill rate (parents where fills > order volume)
metrics.definition: P2 finding B2 / count(parents with at least one fill) * 100.
metrics.rationale: Median over-fill on this dataset is 6.25% (plausible rounding); MAX is 12500.0% (impossible — corruption).

metrics.name: Fill-after-cancel rows
metrics.definition: P2 finding B3. Fill on a parent that was already Cancelled.
metrics.rationale: On REAL data this is a surveillance alert (race condition or fabricated fill). On synthetic data the count is high — likely seeded. The rule is wired correctly either way.

metrics.name: Replace-without-parent rate
metrics.definition: P2 finding B4 / count(Replace Order rows) * 100.
metrics.rationale: Replace is meaningless without the order it's replacing — surveillance can't compare old vs new price/quantity.

metrics.name: Instrument->ISIN drift rate
metrics.definition: P2 finding C1 / total distinct instruments * 100. Instruments mapped to multiple ISINs.
metrics.rationale: HEADLINE DQ FINDING. Per-instrument surveillance rules (price outlier, position aggregation, market-data join) cannot fire reliably until this is resolved.

metrics.name: ISIN->Instrument drift rate
metrics.definition: P2 finding C2 / total distinct ISINs * 100. Reverse mapping.
metrics.rationale: Reverse mapping is stable on this dataset (0%). Reported here so the asymmetry with M5.1 is documented.

metrics.name: Account == CounterPartyFirm collapse rate
metrics.definition: P2 finding D1 / total rows * 100. Account string identical to CPF string.
metrics.rationale: Stage-1 profile shows Account has 1,043 distinct values vs CPF's 100, so granular accounts EXIST but are absent on 97.37% of rows. Looks like an upstream column-copy bug.

metrics.name: Non-PROP self-cross rate
metrics.definition: Subset of M5.3 where Account prefix is not PROP. These are the wash-trade-relevant ones.
metrics.rationale: PROP self-cross is legit; CLNT/BRKR/INST self-cross should be reviewed. Currently masked by M5.3 — fix M5.3 first.

metrics.name: Fill attribution rate (% of fills with Trader)
metrics.definition: 100 - (E1 / count(Fill) * 100). Coverage of Reg-best-execution / MAR attribution.
metrics.rationale: REGULATOR-EXAM CRITICAL. Currently 35.13% — the surveillance system cannot attribute 65% of fills to a human.

metrics.name: New Order attribution rate
metrics.definition: % of New Order rows with Trader populated.
metrics.rationale: Required for spoofing/layering attribution at the parent level.

metrics.name: Cancel Order attribution rate
metrics.definition: % of Cancel Order rows with Trader populated.
metrics.rationale: Required for cancel-ratio-per-trader (F1) to be meaningful.

metrics.name: Replace Order attribution rate
metrics.definition: % of Replace Order rows with Trader populated.
metrics.rationale: Required for modify-then-spoof / quote-stuffing detection.

metrics.name: F2 (wash-trade) eligible-population rate
metrics.definition: Fills with non-null Account + Instrument + parseable timestamp / total Fills * 100.
metrics.rationale: Currently 60,299 of 60,299 fills are eligible. The wash-trade rule cannot fire on the rest.

metrics.name: G1 (price outlier) eligible-population rate
metrics.definition: Rows with Price > 0 + non-null Instrument / total rows * 100.
metrics.rationale: Per-instrument outlier detection has high coverage, but the per-instrument distribution is unreliable until M5.1 (Instrument->ISIN drift) is resolved.

metrics.name: B2 (over-fill) eligible-parent rate
metrics.definition: Parents with TotalVolume + at least one fill with parseable volume / parents with TotalVolume * 100.
metrics.rationale: Parents without fills are ineligible by definition — this metric only counts the rule-relevant denominator.

metrics.name: F1 (cancel ratio) eligible-trader population
metrics.definition: Distinct Traders with at least one New Order AND at least one Cancel Order.
metrics.rationale: F1 is only useful with a tuned threshold AND high Trader coverage (see M6). On this dataset top ratios are 0.68-0.72 — no spoofing pattern seeded.
